myths.statement: Tashigang in Himachal Pradesh is the world's highest polling station.
myths.explanation: Fact: Located at 15,256 feet, Tashigang is officially the highest polling station in the world.

myths.statement: Indelible ink was first used in India's very first election in 1951.
myths.explanation: Fact: It was first introduced in the 3rd General Election in 1962 to prevent double voting.

myths.statement: India uses the 'First-Past-The-Post' system for Lok Sabha elections.
myths.explanation: Fact: The candidate with the most votes in a constituency wins, regardless of whether they have a majority.

myths.statement: The Election Commission of India consists of only one member.
myths.explanation: Fact: It currently consists of the Chief Election Commissioner and two Election Commissioners.

myths.statement: Voters can see their choice printed on paper using VVPAT.
myths.explanation: Fact: Voter Verifiable Paper Audit Trail (VVPAT) allows voters to verify their vote for 7 seconds.

myths.statement: The maximum number of candidates on an EVM is 16.
myths.explanation: Fact: One EVM can handle 16 candidates, but up to 24 units can be linked to support 384 candidates.

myths.statement: The first EVMs were developed by NASA for India.
myths.explanation: Fact: EVMs were designed by the Election Commission in collaboration with Bharat Electronics and ECIL.

myths.statement: A voter can have their name in two different constituency rolls.
myths.explanation: Fact: It is a punishable offense under the RPA Act to be registered in more than one electoral roll.

myths.statement: The symbol of a National Party is reserved for it across all of India.
myths.explanation: Fact: National party symbols (like the Lotus or Hand) cannot be used by anyone else in any state.

myths.statement: Independent candidates are not allowed to contest Lok Sabha elections.
myths.explanation: Fact: Any eligible Indian citizen can contest as an independent candidate.

myths.statement: The total duration of a general election can exceed two months.
myths.explanation: Fact: Due to the scale and security required, Indian elections often span several weeks or months.

myths.statement: Voters in the Gir Forest have a polling booth set up for just one person.
myths.explanation: Fact: For every election, a booth is set up for Mahant Bharatdas Darshandas to ensure 100% participation.
